# Scorched-Earth Erdős Sweep — Turn 5

This is turn 5. Turns 1 through 4 are consolidated in `turns_history__q2_refinement.md` (attached). Re-read it before answering.
Also re-read `mega_prompt.md` and `problem_statement.md`.

CRITICAL — use ONLY the files attached to THIS message. Do NOT open, import, retrieve, or cite any file from your File Library / previously uploaded files, even one named similarly (e.g. another `turns_history…md`). Other unrelated problems use similarly named history files in the same account, and importing one will silently switch you onto the wrong problem. If the attached `turns_history__q2_refinement.md` or `current_manuscript.tex` appears missing, do NOT continue from partial context. Reply only with `MISSING_REQUIRED_ATTACHMENT` and name the missing file(s); never substitute a library file.

If this turn drifts into hammering one specific case or sub-instance, step back and ask whether the case is revealing a structural pattern that would close many cases at once. Prefer arguments whose conclusion is a uniform statement.

End this response with ESTABLISHED / GAP / NEXT + tag.
Continue with Turn 5.

## Turn 5 — proof integrated

The Turn 4 strategy survives, with one essential correction: the shear must be
\[
\Theta_b(A)=A^{S^b},\qquad \Theta_b(S)=S,\qquad \Theta_b(Y)=YS^b,
\]
not \(AS^b\) if the history’s superscript is read literally. Indeed,
\[
[S,YS^b]=[S,Y]^{S^b}
\]
and therefore
\[
\Theta_b\!\left(A^2S^4[S,Y]\right)
 =(A^2)^{S^b}S^4[S,Y]^{S^b}
 =\left(A^2S^4[S,Y]\right)^{S^b}.
\]
Thus it descends to the standard Demushkin group and induces precisely the required abelianized shear \(\bar Y\mapsto\bar Y+b\bar S\).

I integrated the direct argument into the manuscript, replacing the false un-oriented Proposition 3.4 that the original text treated as its sole insertion point.  The downstream proof only requires the resulting fully marked maximal pro-\(2\) identification, so the finite-quotient argument and the displayed candidate relation are unchanged. 

### Structural replacement

For
\[
D_0=\langle A,S,Y\mid A^2S^4[S,Y]=1\rangle_{\mathrm{pro}-2},
\]
write
\[
B=D_0^{\mathrm{ab}},\qquad
t=\bar A+2\bar S,
\qquad
\eta=(-3)^{-1}.
\]
The standard orientation satisfies
\[
\chi_0(t)=-1,\qquad \chi_0(\bar S)=1,\qquad
\chi_0(\bar Y)=\eta.
\]
Since \(v_2(\eta-1)=2\), \(\eta\) generates \(1+4\mathbf Z_2\), and hence
\[
\ker\chi_0=\mathbf Z_2\bar S.
\]
Consequently every element of \(\operatorname{Aut}(B,\chi_0)\) is uniquely
\[
t\mapsto t,\qquad
\bar S\mapsto u\bar S,\qquad
\bar Y\mapsto\bar Y+b\bar S
\]
with \(u\in\mathbf Z_2^\times\) and \(b\in\mathbf Z_2\).

The unit direction is lifted uniformly. Rewriting the relation as
\[
Y^{-1}SY=T,\qquad T=S^{-3}A^{-2},
\]
put
\[
P=S^3,\qquad C=(PT)^{-1}=A^2.
\]
The cyclotomic outer action on the geometric pro-\(2\) fundamental group of
\(\mathbf P^1-\{0,1,\infty\}\) gives, for every \(u\in\mathbf Z_2^\times\), an automorphism of the free pro-\(2\) group on \(P,T\) powering all three peripheral conjugacy classes \(P,T,C\) by \(u\).

It lifts through
\[
E_\square=\langle P,T,A\mid PTA^2=1\rangle_{\mathrm{pro}-2}
          \cong\langle P,A\rangle_{\mathrm{pro}-2}
\]
by sending a conjugate expression for \(C^u\) to the square of the corresponding conjugate of \(A^u\). The Frattini action fixes the free basis, so the lift is an automorphism. Extending across \(Y^{-1}SY=T\) then produces \(\Psi_u\in\operatorname{Aut}(D_0)\). Its abelianized action is some \(\alpha_{u,b(u)}\); the corrected shears \(\Theta_b\) remove the unwanted \(b(u)\). Hence
\[
\operatorname{Aut}(D_0)\twoheadrightarrow\operatorname{Aut}(B,\chi_0).
\]

### Direct marked local normalization

The local reciprocity classes already used by the manuscript satisfy
\[
\bar a=\operatorname{rec}(-4),\qquad
\bar s=\operatorname{rec}(1/2),\qquad
\bar y=\operatorname{rec}(-3),
\]
and give the stated marked abelianization and Hilbert pairing.  I added the missing orientation row
\[
\chi_D(\bar a,\bar s,\bar y)
   =\bigl(-1,1,(-3)^{-1}\bigr).
\]
Labute classification supplies an abstract isomorphism \(f:D\to D_0\). Functoriality of the canonical orientation gives
\[
\chi_0\circ f^{\mathrm{ab}}=\chi_D=\chi_0,
\]
so \(f^{\mathrm{ab}}\in\operatorname{Aut}(B,\chi_0)\). Choose
\[
\gamma\in\operatorname{Aut}(D_0),
\qquad
\gamma^{\mathrm{ab}}=(f^{\mathrm{ab}})^{-1}.
\]
Then \(\beta=\gamma f\) acts identically on \(B\). The elements
\[
a=\beta^{-1}(A),\qquad s=\beta^{-1}(S),\qquad y=\beta^{-1}(Y)
\]
therefore satisfy
\[
a^2s^4[s,y]=1,\qquad
\nu_{\mathrm{ur}}(a,s,y)=(-2,1,0).
\]
This proves Proposition 1.1 without arbitrary-relator normalization, initial-form correction, or lifting an isomorphism back to a free-group automorphism, exactly as anticipated in Turn 4. 

The patched source compiles cleanly to a 40-page PDF with no undefined references:

[Patched LaTeX](sandbox:/mnt/data/current_manuscript_turn5.tex) · [Compiled PDF](sandbox:/mnt/data/current_manuscript_turn5.pdf) · [Unified patch](sandbox:/mnt/data/current_manuscript_turn5.patch)

ESTABLISHED — Proposition 3.4 has been removed and replaced by the uniform surjection \(\operatorname{Aut}(D_0)\twoheadrightarrow\operatorname{Aut}(B,\chi_0)\); the direct marked-isomorphism argument now proves Proposition 1.1, and the candidate presentation is unchanged.

GAP — No internal algebraic gap remains in the replacement argument found in this sweep; publication-level cleanup should add pinpoint references for the canonical-orientation formula in Labute’s odd-rank normal form and for the standard rational-cusp inertia action.

NEXT — Audit those two citations and then perform a final dependency sweep confirming that no obsolete “conditional input” language or reference to the deleted relative-relator proposition survives.

[INTEGRATED]
